headings: h2: Do you need help on local issues? Get in touch today.
homepage.text_excerpt: Andy MacNae MP | Rossendale and Darwen >>>>>>>>> (Gropup 1 - Necessary Cookies) >>>>>>>>> End (Gropup 1 - Necessary Cookies) Find me on Facebook 01254 476410 / 01706 489387 Email Me HOME ABOUT NEWS CAMPAIGNS LINKS CONTACT Do you need help on local issues? Get in touch today. Contact me About Me Rossendale and Darwen Find out more about my work, campaigns and how I can help you with local issues and concerns. READ MORE Campaigns Labour Values Click on the link below to find out more about my campaigns, priorities and Labour policies. READ MORE Follow on Social Media DAILY UPDATES I keep residents updated on social media on my campaigns, news and investment in the local area. FOLLOW ON FACEBOOK Comments Box SVG icons Used for the like, share, comment, and reaction icons Andy MacNae MP 1 day ago Today, the Government have announced a commitment to establish a new National Housing Bank, which will be the government’s investment arm for housing, backed by £16bn of finance. This finance, over its lifetime, will support delivery of over 500,000 new homes, hundreds of thousands of job opportunities in the construction sector, and generate an additional £53bn of additional private investment. £2.5 billion of low-interest loans will be available for social housing providers to increase their capacity to invest in new developments. As part of this announcement, an additional £5bn of capital grant funding will be available for housing, deployed across the country by Homes England and Mayors to support housing delivery. Alongside the work of the Bank, this will deliver grant funding to projects which would otherwise be unviable. It will bring forward large, complex and transformative schemes, including those which require remediation of brownfield sites. This is the kind of long term, joined up thinking I'm very pleased to see our Labour Government engaging in to tackle the housing crisis. ... See More See Less View Comments likes Like love haha 12 Comments: 4 Shares: 5 Share Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on Linked In Share by Email 4 Comments Comment on Facebook just remind rossendale council before they start falling over themselves to give the go ahead to more housing in whitworth WE ARE FULL. without major transport links being improved and a 2nd route through the valley plus schools, dentists and gp places you cant build anything else here on a large scale. Another waist of tax payers money! For who Andy MacNae MP 1 day ago Caitlin Chippendale would make an excellent Borough Councillor for Whitworth. Her strong local connections means she has a deep understanding of the issues affecting Whitworth, and it's clear that she has passion and experience in the realms of helping people and solving problems. Residents of Whitworth have an opportunity on Thursday 26th of June to elect someone who will serve their community tirelessly, and that candidate is Labour's Caitlin Chippendale . Hi, I'm Caitlin Chippendale and I'm your Labour🌹candidate for Whitworth. I'm Rossendale born and bred and went to Whitworth High School, where I was Head Girl. I've been involved in local politics for most of my life because of my Grandad Tom Aldred, a very well known and respected Whitworth Town Councillor, Rossendale Councillor and Alderman. I know the difference that an active and involved local councillor can make to the lives of people here. I remember going out leafletting and campaigning with my grandad when I was very young and I saw for myself the hard work and commitment he put into serving his community and he inspires me to do the same. I'm a problem solver. I work for a charity which rehouses homeless people and I'm with them every step of the way in helping to sort out their problems. I ensure that people get a helping hand when they need it. I'm studying for a maths degree at the Open University and I'd like to become a Maths teacher. I've been working towards this by providing voluntary tuition for local schoolchildren. I'm an active

pages.title: About | Andy MacNae MP
pages.meta_description: Find out more about my work, campaigns and how to help make our local area better for everyone.
pages.text_excerpt: About | Andy MacNae MP >>>>>>>>> (Gropup 1 - Necessary Cookies) >>>>>>>>> End (Gropup 1 - Necessary Cookies) Find me on Facebook 01254 476410 / 01706 489387 Email Me HOME ABOUT NEWS CAMPAIGNS LINKS CONTACT ABOUT Find out more about my work, campaigns and how to help make Rossendale and Darwen better for everyone. ABOUT ME My name is Andy MacNae and I am your Member of Parliament for Rossendale and Darwen. I was elected in 2024 as a Labour Party candidate. I live in Rossendale with my wife and son. Before being elected as your MP, I combined running my adventure sports business with being a local councillor. I have led on local regeneration initiatives including Rawtenstall Town Square, Lee Quarry Mountain Bike Trails, PEER (helping local businesses start and grow) and Bacup Townscape Heritage Initiative. I am a former full-time mountaineer, and I still climb today. Mountain climbing sparked my interest in public service, and I went on to become the national officer and deputy chief executive at the British Mountaineering Council. Another interest of mine is cycling, I ride regularly and want to see more people getting the opportunity to cycle safely, which is why I became a trustee of Cycling UK and in parliament I am treasurer of the All Party Parliamentary Group for Cycling. My objective as an MP is to be an effective champion for Rossendale and Darwen and to deliver on the things that matter to residents. MY PRIORITIES My top priorities as MP for Rossendale and Darwen are: Transport I want to see our communities better connected, with a commuter rail link from Rawtenstall to Manchester, more regular and reliable buses and quicker, safer journeys on the road, free from endless road works. Education Improving access to Special Educational Needs and Disabilities Provision is key to ensuring every child in the constituency is provided with suitable education. Underperforming schools must be improved and apprentices should be available for those who could benefit from them. Healthcare In spite of NHS staff’s best efforts, waiting times are simply too long. I’m pushing for the long-term reforms to the service that we need to turn this situation around, in our local hospitals, GP surgeries and dentists. Crime I’m working alongside local police forces, the Police and Crime Commissioner, and the government to tackle crime and anti-social behaviour across the constituency. Devolution Lancashire is missing out on the incredible opportunities a Manchester-style devolution deal provides. I’m working for Lancashire to have its own Mayor, who would turbo-charge the renewal of our county. Active Lifestyles When we exercise more, we are both happier and healthier. I’m pushing for more accessible, high quality sports facilities in our area, and I’m working on a national level for us all to have improved access to nature. Energy On a national and global level, we must address the threat of climate change through the provision of clean energy. Locally I want to ensure that houses have sufficient insulation and I will be supporting the development of Great British Energy. GET IN TOUCH As your local MP, I can help you with, or offer advice on, issues such as; benefits, immigration, tax, the NHS, child maintenance and education. I would love to hear from you about what you think my priorities should be as your MP and what matters most to you. Please use the contact form to get in touch with me. Please remember to provide as many details as possible, including your name, address and full details of your issue. 01254 47641

pages.title: News | Andy MacNae MP
pages.meta_description: Keep updated on the latest news, campaigns and my work on behalf of voters in the local area.
pages.text_excerpt: News | Andy MacNae MP >>>>>>>>> (Gropup 1 - Necessary Cookies) >>>>>>>>> End (Gropup 1 - Necessary Cookies) Find me on Facebook 01254 476410 / 01706 489387 Email Me HOME ABOUT NEWS SAFER ROADS LINKS CONTACT LATEST NEWS Keep updated on the latest news, campaigns and my work on behalf of voters in the local area. Comments Box SVG icons Used for the like, share, comment, and reaction icons Andy MacNae MP 17 hours ago Great day out on the doorstep with the team #VoteLabour Out and about campaigning around Redvers Rd area for our fantastic candidate in Darwen East ward Councillor Martin Mccaughran. ... See More See Less View Comments likes Like love 31 Comments: 4 Shares: 1 Share Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on Linked In Share by Email 4 Comments Comment on Facebook Andy MacNae MP 18 hours ago Fantastic visit to Sudell Primary School in Darwen 🏫 It is brilliant to see how the school continues to build on its success year after year, backed by the excellent support of Aldridge Education. During my visit, we had some highly productive discussions about a possible expansion of the school's offering, as well as how Darwen’s £20 million Pride in Place funding from central government will help level up and benefit our local community. 💷🏘️ The highlight of the day, however, was definitely facing the Year 6 students! They kept me firmly on my toes with some incredibly sharp and varied questions covering: 📚 SATs 👔 School uniforms 🎓 The wider education system ✈️ Term-time holidays It’s always a privilege to hear directly from our bright young minds—they certainly didn't hold back. A massive thank you to the staff and students at Sudell Primary for such a warm welcome. ... See More See Less View Comments likes Like love 15 Comments: 3 Shares: 2 Share Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on Linked In Share by Email 3 Comments Comment on Facebook Andy MacNae MP 2 days ago Rossendale Hospice does outstanding work for families in our community during their most difficult times, but today's visit highlighted the immense financial pressure they are under. It is vital we step up to help. I have pledged my full support for local fundraising and will be actively backing Hospice UK’s national campaign for fair funding. Take a look at the Hospice UK campaign and see why this matters so much: www.hospiceuk.org/our-campaigns/fair-funding-for-hospices ... See More See Less View Comments likes Like love haha angry 53 Comments: 11 Shares: 8 Share Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on Linked In Share by Email 11 Comments Comment on Facebook Andy MacNae MP 3 days ago I convened a roundtable of car insurers, including Aviva, Admiral and Confused.com, to discuss how we can encourage young drivers to drive safely and responsibly, which could in turn make insurance cheaper. Getting this right is vital, as although only 6% of all drivers are under the age of 25, they make up a quarter of all serious collisions. We will be sharing the findings directly with the Government. ... See More See Less View Comments likes Like love haha 38 Comments: 23 Shares: 4 Share Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on Linked In Share by Email 23 Comments Comment on Facebook Andy MacNae MP 4 days ago Recent reporting has exposed Reform's deeply offensive, racist, and homophobic posts from their local candidate. Instead of suspending him, local party officials have defended this as "freedom of speech." Today, I wrote to the leadership of Reform to demand action. Racism, hatred against women and homophobia have no place in our community or our democracy. Read the full report from the Lancashire Telegraph : 🔗 www.lancashiretelegraph.co.uk/news/26040212.blackburn-reform-uk-candidate-offensive-posts-spark-row/ ... See More See Less View Comments likes Like love haha wow sad angry 557 Comments: 712 Shares: 11 Share Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on Linked In Share by Email 712 Comments Comment on Facebook Andy MacNae MP 5 days ago Enough is enough!

Facebook X-twitter Envelope ECONOMIC GROWTH GET BRITAIN BUILDING AGAIN Good jobs and productivity growth in every part of the country making everyone, not just a few, better off. Details CLEAN ENERGY Great British Energy British people are facing challenges with higher bills. The only way to cut energy bills is to shift to clean, low carbon power. Details NHS & HEALTH Get the NHS back on its feet There when people need it; with fewer lives lost to the biggest killers; in a fairer Britain, where everyone lives well for longer. Details SAFER STREETS Take back our streets Labour believes that feeling secure is the bedrock on which opportunities are built, and communities can thrive. Details OPPORTUNITY barriers to opportunity Reform the childcare and education systems, raise standards everywhere, and prepare young people for work and life. Details HELP US WIN VOLUNTEERING WITH LABOUR Be part of the team that delivers a Labour government. Over 50,000 people have already pledged. pages.meta_description: Would you like to find out more about my campaigns? Please use the contact form below to get in touch.
pages.text_excerpt: Contact | Andy MacNae MP >>>>>>>>> (Gropup 1 - Necessary Cookies) >>>>>>>>> End (Gropup 1 - Necessary Cookies) Find me on Facebook 01254 476410 / 01706 489387 Email Me HOME ABOUT NEWS CAMPAIGNS LINKS CONTACT CONTACT Would you like to find out more about my campaigns and get help on local issues? Please use the contact form below to get in touch. I can help residents with various issues, such as education, environmental issues, antisocial behaviour, health and social services, highways, transport, and housing. By contrast, your Councillors can help constituents on various other issues, such as council tax, bin collections, housing issues, social services, schools and roads and payments. You can use the contact form here to get in touch with me if you require my assistance. Please remember to provide as many details as possible, including your name, address, telephone number and issue. Alternatively, you can write to us at: Andy MacNae MP, House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA. 01254 476410 / 01706 489387 andy.macnae.mp@parliament.uk Are you human? * This helps us prevent spam, thank you.
